title: HTML Tables
description: Create structured data tables with semantic markup

lessons.title: Data Tables
lessons.description:
Tables display structured data in rows and columns. Use <table> as the container, <tr> for rows, <th> for header cells, and <td> for data cells.

Add <caption> for an accessible title that describes the table's content.
lessons.task:
Create a pricing table:
1. A <caption> saying Pricing
2. A header row with Plan and Price
3. Two data rows for Basic ($9) and Pro ($29)
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui; padding: 20px; background: #f5f5f5; } table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; max-width: 350px; background: white; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } caption { padding: 16px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.1rem; color: #333; background: #f8f9fa; } th, td { padding: 14px 20px; text-align: left; border-bottom: 1px solid #eee; } th { background: steelblue; color: white; font-weight: 500; } tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; } tr:hover td { background: #f8f9fa; }

validations.message: Add a <kbd><caption></kbd> for the table title
Add header cells (<kbd><th></kbd>) for Plan and Price

lessons.title: Table Sections
lessons.description:
Semantic table sections improve accessibility and allow for separate styling:

• <thead> — header section
• <tbody> — main content
• <tfoot> — footer (totals, summaries)
lessons.task: Wrap the header row in <kbd><thead></kbd> and data rows in <kbd><tbody></kbd>.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui; padding: 20px; } table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; max-width: 350px; background: white; border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } th, td { padding: 12px 16px; text-align: left; } thead { background: steelblue; color: white; } tbody tr:nth-child(even) { background: #f8f9fa; }

validations.message: Add a <kbd><thead></kbd> section for the header
Add a <kbd><tbody></kbd> section for the data

lessons.title: Spanning Columns
lessons.description:
The colspan attribute lets a cell span multiple columns. This is useful for headers that group multiple columns or footer totals.

<td colspan="2">...</td>
lessons.task: Add a footer row that spans both columns using <kbd>colspan="2"</kbd>.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui; padding: 20px; } table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; max-width: 350px; background: white; border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } th, td { padding: 12px 16px; text-align: left; border-bottom: 1px solid #eee; } thead { background: steelblue; color: white; } tfoot { background: #f0f0f0; font-weight: 600; }
